name: Seattle
lifestyle: Pacific Northwest tech hub with no state income tax, temperate climate, and strong Asia connectivity. Housing is meaningfully cheaper than SF or NYC, and the zero-state-income-tax advantage compounds over a Fat FIRE horizon. Residency establishment is straightforward versus high-tax exit states.

true_fat_fire.description: Belltown/Downtown high-rise 2-3BR with view, weekly housekeeping, Canlis-tier dining monthly, 3+ international trips plus domestic ski/BC weekends.

guide.groceries: Whole Foods, PCC, and specialty butcher or fish-counter shopping with premium produce, seafood, wine, and easy entertaining at home.
guide.dining: Several good dinners out most weeks plus destination dining regularly, with nicer nights often around $150-$300 per person before wine.
guide.rhythm: Ride-hail when needed, housekeeping support, boutique fitness or wellness, and a leisure pattern that includes Whistler, islands, and long-haul travel.

comfortable_expat.description: Capitol Hill/Fremont/Ballard 1BR apartment, dining out 2-3 nights/week, bike and transit, summer PNW trips plus 1-2 international annually.

guide.groceries: A mix of Trader Joe's, PCC, farmers' market produce, and decent seafood or meat for a strong at-home kitchen without going full luxury.
guide.dining: Dining out 2-3 times a week plus the occasional nicer dinner, usually around $25-$50 casually and $100-$180 per person on a bigger night.
guide.rhythm: Mostly transit, bike, or occasional ride-hail, moderate wellness spending, and weekends shaped around hikes, ferries, and short regional escapes.

luxury_family.description: Mercer Island or Bellevue 4BR house, top private school access (Lakeside/Bush/Eastside Prep) when dependents apply, annual international and ski trips, occasional housekeeper.

guide.groceries: Large family basket through PCC, Whole Foods, Costco-style top-ups, and premium proteins, snacks, and school provisioning without much compromise.
guide.dining: Family brunches and dinners out most weekends plus adult meals, with casual family meals often $30-$60 per person and nicer nights materially above that.
guide.rhythm: Housekeeping or childcare help, school and activity logistics, and a calendar built around skiing, camps, and international trips during breaks.
